machine.name: D3D v18.10
machine.displayName: D3D v18.10 - Full FreeCAD Assembly: Frame, Axes, Z-Axis, Extruder, Heated Bed, and Cable Management
machine.description: Complete D3D v18.10 assembly covering all 61 FreeCAD-sourced parts plus 32 procedural parts. Stages: frame fabrication and cube joining, Y/X axis composite units and their internal decomposition (motors, idlers, rods, carriages, brackets), X-axis drive, Z-axis vertical motion columns (front and back), three extruder stages, heated bed mechanical stack with bed platform (raisers, guide rods, Y-bed rods), and cable management routing.
learningObjectives: Recognize the stock dimensions used to fabricate the D3D frame sides
Place all 24 flat bars in their real 14 inch square side geometry
Preserve the 1 inch overlap that converts 13 inch bar stock into a 14 inch outer square
Check each side for squareness before heat is introduced
Clamp and tack-weld each verified side while it remains pinned flat
Place the finished frame-side panels into their correct cube positions
Check, hold, tack, seam-weld, clean up, and recheck the stacked cube before motion hardware is added
Mount the first Y-left and Y-right axis units to the accepted frame
Fit the first X-axis module tightly between the completed Y-axis pair
Lock, tension, and check the first X-axis module without visible gap or binding
Build the first Titan Aero nozzle, fan, and sensor module before later carriage integration
Assemble the carriage-side Titan Aero bracket, top plate, and simplified carriage stack before final printer-wide extruder mounting is introduced
Mount the completed extruder carriage unit onto the X-axis carriage interface and close the half-carriage clamp before later heated-bed and wiring modules are authored
Stage the first mechanical heated-bed body and retainer pair before later heater wiring and control integration are introduced
prerequisiteNotes: The six panel work zones are a tighter raised teaching layout so small real-size stock remains readable and selectable while preserving exact panel geometry.
This package now covers frame fabrication, cube joining, and a first axes stage.
Axes Stage 01 uses source-backed procedure order and mounting relationships, but the axis module shapes remain schematic placeholders until exact v18.10 axis dimensions are extracted.
Extruder Stage 01 locks the Titan Aero path and stops at the nozzle / fan / sensor module before the unresolved v18.10 carriage-mount variant is authored.
Extruder Stage 02 assembles the carriage-side bracket, top plate, and simplified carriage stack only. Final printer-wide mounted orientation, spacer usage, and wiring remain deferred.
Extruder Stage 03 mounts the completed carriage unit onto the X-axis carriage interface and closes the half-carriage clamp. The spacer and finer closure-fastener story remain deferred.
Heated Bed Stage 01 is mechanical-only: the bed body and snapbuckle pair are authored now, while wirelock, heater wiring, and later control/electronics details remain deferred.

assemblies.name: D3D Frame
assemblies.description: Complete set of six D3D frame sides authored as source-backed layup, clamp-up, tack-welded panels, stacked cube targets, bracket-based cube joining with staged bolt tightening, post-bracket square verification, and frame acceptance before motion hardware.
assemblies.learningFocus: Repeatable frame-side geometry, square verification, stable hold-down, correct spatial stacking of the finished frame sides into the cube, bracket-based cube joining with staged bolt tightening, square verification between stages, and the acceptance logic used before motion hardware is added.

assemblies.name: D3D Axes Stage 01
assemblies.description: Mounts the first Y-left and Y-right axis units to the completed frame, then seats, fits, tensions, and checks the first X-axis module between them.
assemblies.learningFocus: Direct frame mounting of the first Y-axis pair, the source-backed X-axis fitting order, constrained span adjustment, belt tensioning, and the first motion-stage checks.

assemblies.name: D3D Extruder Stage 01
assemblies.description: Stages the Titan Aero core, attaches the nozzle assembly, builds the first fan and sensor-side attachments, inserts the 8 mm sensor, and verifies clearance before later carriage integration.
assemblies.learningFocus: Titan Aero module decomposition, nozzle-side extruder sequencing, first fan and sensor-side attachments, and a conservative service-clearance check before the unresolved carriage-mount variant is introduced.

assemblies.name: D3D Extruder Stage 02
assemblies.description: Stages the simplified carriage, assembles the first carriage-side Titan Aero bracket and top-plate stack, mounts the completed nozzle module onto that carriage-side stack, and checks service clearance before later printer-wide integration.
assemblies.learningFocus: Carriage-side extruder mount-stack assembly, bracket and top-plate fastening order, rigid nozzle-module mounting, and service-clearance checks before later printer-wide extruder integration.

assemblies.name: D3D Extruder Stage 03
assemblies.description: Stages the X-axis carriage-side interface, mounts the completed extruder carriage unit onto it, closes the half-carriage clamp, and checks X-axis travel clearance before later heated-bed and wiring work.
assemblies.learningFocus: Printer-side extruder integration, carriage-side closure with the half carriage, and first-pass X-travel/nozzle-clearance verification.

assemblies.name: D3D Heated Bed Stage 01
assemblies.description: Stages the first mechanical heated-bed body into the printer, places the snapbuckle retainer pair, and checks first bed-envelope / nozzle clearance before heater wiring and control work are introduced.
assemblies.learningFocus: Mechanical bed-body placement, first retainer placement, and first bed-envelope / nozzle-clearance review before any heated-bed wiring is authored.

assemblies.name: D3D Axes Stage 02 (Axis Internals)
assemblies.description: Decomposes the pre-assembled Y-axis composite units into individual internal components and adds the X-axis drive motor and rods. This stage exposes the internal sub-parts that make up each axis module.

assemblies.name: D3D Z-Axis Stage 01
assemblies.description: Vertical motion system for the X-axis gantry. Two Z columns (front and back) each contain a stepper motor, motor mount, leadscrew idler, carriage, and spacers.

assemblies.name: D3D Heated Bed Stage 02 (Bed Platform)
assemblies.description: Bed platform assembly: six standoff raisers, two bed guide rods, and three Y-axis bed travel rods. The platform rides on the Y carriages and supports the heated bed at the correct height.

assemblies.name: D3D Cable Management
assemblies.description: Cable chain routing for X-axis and Y-axis wiring. Protects wiring from motion-induced wear and keeps cables organized during printer operation.

tools.name: Framing Square
tools.purpose: Confirms that each D3D frame side stays square before clamping and tack welding.
tools.usageNotes: Seat both legs of the square against adjacent flat bars before any tack weld is placed.
tools.defaultOrientationHint: Hold the inside corner of the square against the bar intersection.
tools.safetyNotes: Keep the work surface clear so the square sits flat against both bars.

tools.name: Locking Clamp
tools.purpose: Pins a verified frame side flat so the overlapped bars cannot shift while tack welds are added.
tools.usageNotes: Clamp near the panel center after the square check passes, then keep the bars flat while you tack opposite corners.
tools.safetyNotes: Keep fingers clear of the jaws and release clamping force slowly.

tools.name: MIG Torch
tools.purpose: Places short tack welds at the overlapped frame-side corners.
tools.usageNotes: Use brief tacks in the authored order so the panel stays square and heat input remains controlled.
tools.safetyNotes: Use welding PPE, protect nearby surfaces, and maintain ventilation before striking an arc.

tools.name: Angle Grinder
tools.purpose: Cleans proud weld metal and spatter from the frame faces that will later carry rail and bracket hardware.
tools.usageNotes: Use light cleanup passes on the authored upper joints so later mounting faces stay usable without removing structural material.
tools.safetyNotes: Use eye and face protection, keep the guard in place, and control sparks away from the work area.

tools.name: 5 mm Metric Allen Key
tools.purpose: Starts and tightens the X-axis fitting fasteners.

tools.name: Pliers
tools.purpose: Pulls the X-axis belt to final tension.

tools.name: 2.5 mm Metric Hex Key
tools.purpose: Secures the small holder-side fasteners during the first Titan Aero module build.

tools.name: Small Screwdriver
tools.purpose: Secures the blower attachment during the first Titan Aero module build.

steps.name: Place all four bottom frame side bars
steps.instructionText: Place all four bars of the bottom frame side into their target positions. Keep each bar flat and preserve the 1 inch overlap that produces the 14 inch outer square.
steps.whyItMattersText: All four edges of the bottom frame side must stay true to the 14 inch square geometry or the final D3D frame will accumulate twist before assembly.

guidance.instructionText: Place all four bars of the bottom frame side into their target positions. Keep each bar flat and preserve the 1 inch overlap that produces the 14 inch outer square.
guidance.whyItMattersText: All four edges of the bottom frame side must stay true to the 14 inch square geometry or the final D3D frame will accumulate twist before assembly.

steps.name: Verify the bottom frame side is square
steps.instructionText: Equip the framing square and check the upper-left corner marker on the bottom frame side before any clamp load or heat is introduced.
steps.whyItMattersText: Verifying the bottom frame side before welding catches layout error while the bars can still be shifted freely.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Bottom Frame Side checked for square.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and use it on the pulsing corner marker.

guidance.instructionText: Equip the framing square and check the upper-left corner marker on the bottom frame side before any clamp load or heat is introduced.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Verifying the bottom frame side before welding catches layout error while the bars can still be shifted freely.

steps.name: Clamp the bottom frame side
steps.instructionText: Equip the locking clamp and pin the verified bottom frame side at the center hold-down marker so the bars stay flat while the corner tacks go in.
steps.whyItMattersText: Stable hold-down prevents the overlapped bars from walking out of square once tack weld heat is added.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Bottom Frame Side pinned flat for tack welding.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the locking clamp and use it on the pulsing hold-down marker.

guidance.instructionText: Equip the locking clamp and pin the verified bottom frame side at the center hold-down marker so the bars stay flat while the corner tacks go in.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Stable hold-down prevents the overlapped bars from walking out of square once tack weld heat is added.

steps.name: Tack-weld the bottom frame side corners
steps.instructionText: Equip the MIG torch and place four short tack welds on the bottom frame side in this order: upper-left, lower-right, upper-right, then lower-left. Keep the panel flat and avoid a long heat soak.
steps.whyItMattersText: Short tacks lock the overlap geometry in place while limiting distortion before the full frame cube is assembled.

steps.name: Place the bottom frame side
steps.instructionText: Move the completed bottom frame side from its fabrication station to the cube bottom target.
steps.whyItMattersText: The bottom panel establishes the base footprint for the remaining frame sides.

steps.name: Place the left frame side
steps.instructionText: Lift the completed left frame side and place it on the left cube target.
steps.whyItMattersText: The left panel establishes one of the first vertical faces of the printer frame.

steps.name: Place the right frame side
steps.instructionText: Lift the completed right frame side and place it on the right cube target.
steps.whyItMattersText: The right panel locks the cube width opposite the left face.

steps.name: Place the front frame side
steps.instructionText: Lift the completed front frame side and place it on the front cube target.
steps.whyItMattersText: The front panel starts closing the cube depth and establishes the front opening plane.

steps.name: Place the rear frame side
steps.instructionText: Lift the completed rear frame side and place it on the rear cube target.
steps.whyItMattersText: The rear panel completes the four vertical faces before the top closes the frame.

steps.name: Place the top frame side
steps.instructionText: Move the completed top frame side to the upper cube target to close the frame layout.
steps.whyItMattersText: The top panel completes the first-pass cube stack and exposes any remaining alignment issues before final joining.

steps.name: Verify the stacked cube is square
steps.instructionText: Equip the framing square and check the top-front-left cube corner after all six panels are stacked but before the frame is clamped and tacked together.
steps.whyItMattersText: This catches accumulated twist or panel drift before the first cube tacks lock the assembled frame.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Stacked cube checked for square.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and use it on the highlighted cube corner marker.

steps.name: Place 4 lower corner brackets
steps.instructionText: Position a 3D-printed corner bracket at each of the four lower cube corners. Align the bracket holes with the frame sides and start two M6Ã—18 bolts per bracket finger-tight. Work in this order: front-left, rear-right, front-right, then rear-left.
steps.whyItMattersText: Starting with the lower corners establishes the base geometry of the cube before the upper corners are locked in.

requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the allen key and place the bracket at the highlighted lower corner.

steps.name: Snug lower bracket bolts finger-tight
steps.instructionText: Use the allen key to snug all 8 bolts on the four lower brackets to finger-tight. Do not fully tighten yet â€” the frame needs to stay adjustable for the upper brackets.
steps.whyItMattersText: Leaving bolts finger-tight allows minor adjustments when the upper brackets are added, ensuring the cube stays square.

steps.name: Place 4 upper corner brackets
steps.instructionText: Position a corner bracket at each of the four upper cube corners. Start two M6Ã—18 bolts per bracket finger-tight. Work in the same order: front-left, rear-right, front-right, then rear-left.
steps.whyItMattersText: Upper brackets complete the structural lock of all eight cube corners.

steps.name: Snug upper bracket bolts finger-tight
steps.instructionText: Use the allen key to snug all 8 bolts on the four upper brackets to finger-tight. The cube should now hold its shape loosely with all 8 brackets in place.
steps.whyItMattersText: With all brackets finger-tight the cube can still be nudged square before the final tightening pass.

steps.name: Verify bracketed cube is square
steps.instructionText: Equip the framing square and check the top-front-left cube corner. Tap any out-of-square sides into alignment while the bolts are still finger-tight.
steps.whyItMattersText: This is the last chance to adjust the cube geometry before the bolts are fully tightened.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Bracketed cube checked for square.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and check the highlighted cube corner.

steps.name: Final-tighten all bracket bolts
steps.instructionText: Use the allen key to fully tighten all 16 bracket bolts across all 8 corners. Work in alternating diagonal pairs (lower front-left then upper rear-right, etc.) to distribute clamping force evenly.
steps.whyItMattersText: Even tightening prevents one corner from pulling the frame out of square while the opposite corner is still loose.

steps.name: Verify cube after final tightening
steps.instructionText: Equip the framing square and recheck the top-front-left cube corner after all bolts are fully tightened. Confirm the frame stayed true through the tightening sequence.
steps.whyItMattersText: A post-tightening square check catches any shift introduced by bolt clamping forces.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Tightened cube checked for square.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and check the highlighted cube corner.

steps.name: Recheck opposite cube corners
steps.instructionText: Equip the framing square and verify both the top-front-left and top-rear-right cube corners. This cross-check confirms the cube is true on opposite diagonals.
steps.whyItMattersText: Checking opposite corners catches twist that a single-corner check might miss.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Front-left corner rechecked.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and check the highlighted corner.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Rear-right corner rechecked.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the framing square and check the highlighted corner.

steps.name: Accept the frame for motion hardware
steps.instructionText: Make a final acceptance check on the bracketed frame and continue only when it is ready for the first rail, gantry, and motion-hardware installation steps.
steps.whyItMattersText: Closing the frame phase explicitly helps prevent structural errors from propagating into the next subsystem.

steps.name: Mount the Y-left axis to the frame
steps.instructionText: Place the completed Y-left axis unit onto the welded frame at the authored left-side mount.

steps.name: Confirm the Y-left axis seats cleanly on the frame
steps.instructionText: Inspect the mounted Y-left axis and confirm its travel direction is clear and the unit sits flat on the frame-side mount.

steps.name: Mount the Y-right axis to the frame
steps.instructionText: Place the completed Y-right axis unit onto the welded frame at the authored right-side mount.

steps.name: Confirm the Y-axis pair establishes the X-axis span
steps.instructionText: Inspect the mounted Y-left and Y-right axes together and confirm they establish a clean span for the X-axis between them.

steps.name: Tighten the X-axis idler screws
steps.instructionText: Tighten the X-axis idler screws while the module is still staged in the work area.

steps.name: Place the X-axis assembly with the idler side anchored on Y-right
steps.instructionText: Place the X-axis idler side, rod pair, motor-holder side, and the two Y-right M6x30 bolts into their pre-fit positions so the idler side anchors on Y-right first.

steps.name: Snug the Y-right M6x30 anchor bolts
steps.instructionText: Use the metric Allen key to snug the two Y-right M6x30 bolts so the idler side stays anchored while the motor-holder side still remains adjustable.

steps.name: Loosely start the X-axis motor-holder bolts on the Y-left carriage
steps.instructionText: Place the two Y-left M6x30 motor-holder bolts by hand only loosely so the X-axis span can still be adjusted.

steps.name: Lengthen the X-axis to fit tightly between the Y axes
steps.instructionText: Drag the yellow motor-holder side left along the fit guide until it reaches the green final-fit marker and seats against the Y-left side.

steps.name: Lock the Y-left mount bolts and motor-holder screws
steps.instructionText: After the X-axis span is seated tightly, tighten the two Y-left M6x30 mount bolts first, then lock the motor-holder-side internal screws.

steps.name: Tension the X-axis belt
steps.instructionText: Pull the X-axis belt to final hand tension only after the span and fasteners are locked.

steps.name: Reinsert the belt peg
steps.instructionText: Place the belt peg back into the retainer once the belt is tensioned.

steps.name: Check X-axis tightness and travel
steps.instructionText: Inspect the fitted X-axis and confirm it can travel along the mounted Y axes without binding, without a visible gap at the left interface, and with the Y-left end-stop relationship preserved.

steps.name: Stage the Titan Aero core
steps.instructionText: Place the Titan Aero core into the extruder work zone so the nozzle, fan, and sensor-side parts can be added in sequence.

steps.name: Attach the nozzle assembly
steps.instructionText: Place the nozzle assembly onto the staged Titan Aero core before any cooling or sensor-side hardware is added.

steps.name: Place the print-cooling blower
steps.instructionText: Place the 5015 blower on the fan side of the nozzle module before securing its mount points.

steps.name: Secure the blower attachment
steps.instructionText: Tighten the blower-side fasteners only after the blower body is seated on the nozzle module.

steps.name: Place the sensor holder
steps.instructionText: Place the sensor holder on the nozzle module after the blower side is established.

steps.name: Secure the sensor holder
steps.instructionText: Tighten the small holder-side fasteners so the sensor holder is fixed before the sensor is inserted.

steps.name: Place the 8 mm sensor
steps.instructionText: Place the 8 mm sensor into the holder after the holder is secured.

steps.name: Check nozzle-module clearance
steps.instructionText: Inspect the completed nozzle module and confirm the nozzle, blower side, and sensor side all keep clear service space for later carriage integration.

steps.name: Stage the simplified carriage
steps.instructionText: Place the simplified carriage into the carriage-mount work zone before the printed bracket and top plate are added.

steps.name: Place the Titan Aero mount bracket
steps.instructionText: Place the stock Titan Aero mount bracket onto the staged simplified carriage before any fasteners are tightened.

steps.name: Secure the mount bracket
steps.instructionText: Use the metric Allen key to tighten the two bracket fasteners only after the bracket sits flat on the simplified carriage.

steps.name: Place the Titan Aero mount top plate
steps.instructionText: Place the top plate over the bracket stack so the carriage-side mount can be locked before the completed nozzle module is added.

steps.name: Secure the mount top plate
steps.instructionText: Use the small hex driver to tighten the top-plate fasteners so the bracket stack is locked before the completed nozzle module is mounted.

steps.name: Mount the completed nozzle module to the carriage stack
steps.instructionText: Move the completed Titan Aero nozzle module onto the prepared carriage-side bracket stack. Keep the module square to the mount and let the rigid subassembly target guide the final seated pose.

steps.name: Check carriage-mount clearance and access
steps.instructionText: Inspect the completed carriage-side Titan Aero mount and confirm the body, nozzle, and sensor side all keep basic service access before later printer-wide integration is authored.

steps.name: Stage the X-axis carriage side
steps.instructionText: Place the X-axis carriage-side interface onto the rod-pair work area so the completed extruder carriage unit has a real printer-side receiving body.

steps.name: Mount the completed extruder carriage unit onto the X-axis interface
steps.instructionText: Move the completed extruder carriage unit onto the X-axis carriage-side interface and let the rigid mount target guide the first seated printer-side position.

steps.name: Close the half carriage over the mounted extruder unit
steps.instructionText: Bring the half carriage in square to the mounted carriage side so the clamp-side body closes over the rod path and captures the mounted extruder unit.

steps.name: Check extruder X-axis travel clearance
steps.instructionText: Inspect the mounted extruder carriage and confirm it can move along the X-axis without visible interference at the left and right travel limits and with nozzle-side clearance preserved.

steps.name: Stage the heatbed body
steps.instructionText: Place the heatbed body into the printer so the first mechanical bed envelope is established before the retainers are added.

steps.name: Place the left snapbuckle
steps.instructionText: Place the left snapbuckle retainer onto the left bed-side contact point after the heatbed body is seated.

steps.name: Place the right snapbuckle
steps.instructionText: Place the right snapbuckle retainer onto the right bed-side contact point so the first mechanical bed path is held symmetrically.

steps.name: Check heated-bed envelope clearance
steps.instructionText: Inspect the staged heatbed body and retainer pair and confirm the bed envelope stays centered under the nozzle path with clear left and right margins.

steps.name: Assembly complete
steps.instructionText: All parts are now in their final assembled positions. Rotate the view to inspect the completed D3D printer stage 1 assembly.

steps.name: Place Y-Left Axis Internals
steps.instructionText: Position the Y-left axis internal components: carriage plate, belt idler, stepper motor, guide rods, endstop sensor, and mounting bracket. These are the sub-parts inside the pre-assembled Y-left axis unit.

steps.name: Place Y-Right Axis Internals
steps.instructionText: Position the Y-right axis internal components: carriage plate, belt idler, stepper motor, guide rods, and mounting bracket. These mirror the Y-left axis on the opposite side of the frame.

steps.name: Place X-Axis Drive
steps.instructionText: Position the X-axis stepper motor and guide rods. The motor mounts on the left side of the X-axis module and the rods span horizontally between the Y carriages.

steps.name: Place Z-Axis Front Column
steps.instructionText: Position the front Z-axis column components: stepper motor at the top, motor mount, carriage, lower idler, and spacers. This column provides the front vertical motion for the X-axis gantry.

steps.name: Place Z-Axis Back Column
steps.instructionText: Position the back Z-axis column components: stepper motor, motor mount, carriage, lower idler, and spacers. This column mirrors the front column on the opposite side.

steps.name: Place Bed Platform
steps.instructionText: Position the six bed raiser standoffs and two bed guide rods. The raisers support the heated bed at the correct height above the Z carriages, and the guide rods allow smooth Y-axis travel.

steps.name: Place Y-Axis Bed Rods
steps.instructionText: Position the Y-axis bed guide rods connecting the front and back Z carriages. These rods enable the bed platform to travel smoothly along the Y axis.

steps.name: Place Cable Management
steps.instructionText: Position the cable chain assemblies: X-axis cable chain at the top of the gantry, Y-axis cable chain along the left side, and the cable chain bracket that anchors the Y chain to the Y-left carriage.

hints.title: Recheck the welded cube
hints.message: Use the framing square on the highlighted cube corner after the seam passes to confirm the frame stayed square.

hints.title: Lower bracket placement order
hints.message: Place brackets at the four lower cube corners in diagonal order: front-left, rear-right, front-right, rear-left. Start two M6 bolts per bracket but leave them loose.

hints.title: Upper bracket placement order
hints.message: Place brackets at the four upper cube corners in the same diagonal order. Start two M6 bolts per bracket but leave them loose.

hints.title: Finger-tight only
hints.message: Snug the bolts just enough to hold the bracket in place. Do not fully tighten until all 8 brackets are placed and the cube has been checked for square.

hints.title: Diagonal tightening pattern
hints.message: Tighten bolts in alternating diagonal pairs to distribute clamping force evenly. For example: lower front-left, then upper rear-right, then lower front-right, then upper rear-left.
